title: Python Basics
subtitle: Build your first Python instincts, one tiny mission at a time.

questions.prompt: Which symbol starts a comment in Python?
questions.explanation: Python ignores everything after a # on the same line, so it is used for comments.
questions.commonMistake: Many beginners use // because they have seen it in JavaScript or Java, but Python comments start with #.

questions.explanation: The print function sends text or values to the screen.

questions.hint: Look for the option wrapped in quote marks. Quotes usually signal text in Python.
questions.explanation: Text wrapped in quotes is a string. Both single and double quotes work in Python.
Multiplication happens before addition, so 3 * 4 is 12, then 2 + 12 is 14.
questions.commonMistake: A common mistake is adding 2 + 3 first. Python follows operator precedence, so multiplication runs before addition.

questions.prompt: What is a known risk of using a mutable value (like a list) as a default function argument in Python?
options: The default value is shared across all function calls
Python throws a syntax error on definition
The function cannot accept any other arguments
questions.hint: Think about when default values are evaluated—only once at function definition, or every time the function runs?
questions.explanation: In Python, default arguments are evaluated once when the function is defined. If you use a mutable default like a list, it is shared across all calls, which can cause unexpected accumulations of data.
questions.commonMistake: Beginners often expect a new list to be created every time the function is called. Instead, you should default to None and initialize the list inside the function.

questions.prompt: What is the output of [x * 2 for x in [1, 2, 3, 4] if x % 2 == 0]?
questions.hint: Evaluate the if filter condition first on the input list, then apply the multiplication x * 2 to the matching elements.
questions.explanation: The list comprehension filters the list first, keeping only even numbers (2 and 4), then multiplies each by 2 to yield [4, 8].
questions.commonMistake: A common mistake is applying the operation x * 2 before filtering, or missing the filter condition entirely.

questions.explanation: len returns the number of items in a collection. This list has three items.

questions.prompt: Which keyword starts a conditional branch?
questions.explanation: Python uses if to run code only when a condition is true.

questions.prompt: Which loop is best for visiting every item in a list?
questions.hint: For a list, think about the loop form that names one item at a time.
questions.explanation: A for loop can visit each item in a list using syntax like for item in items:.
input reads what the user types and returns it as a string.
